tex:
24 people own a car.
\newline

(a) 
Ranjit asks each of these 24 people the colour of their car.
\newline

The bar chart shows some of these results.
\newline

(i)  Complete the bar chart by drawing the bar for the colour red.
\newline

 Write down the mode.
\newline

 How many more people have a green car than have a blue car?

The table shows the size, in litres, of each of the 24 car engines.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Engine size (litres) & 0.8 & 1.2 & 1.5 & 1.8 & 2.4 \\
\hline
Frequency & 7 & 4 & 5 & 6 & 2 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
Calculate the mean.
\newline

Ranjit also asks each of the 24 people what type of car they have.
\newline

The pie chart shows some of these results.
\newline

(i)  Work out the number of hybrid cars.
\newline

 The rest of the cars are either petrol or diesel.
\newline

There are 8 petrol cars.
\newline

Complete the pie chart to show this information.
\newline

qtype: percentage increase (tax), time calculation, average speed
tex:
Mr and Mrs Perez and their 3 children go on holiday to Tokyo.
\newline

(a) 
The holiday costs \$3800 for an adult and \$2400 for a child.
\newline

8\% tax is then added to the cost of the holiday.
\newline

Find the total cost of the holiday, including tax, for the Perez family.
\newline

The plane takes 11 hours 40 minutes to fly from Los Angeles to Tokyo.
\newline

The plane leaves on Wednesday at 10$\,$35 local time.
\newline

The local time in Tokyo is 17 hours ahead of the local time in Los Angeles.
\newline

(i)  Find the day and local time in Tokyo when the plane arrives.
\newline

{---}   Time ][]

 The distance the plane flies is $8820\,\text{km}$.
\newline

Calculate the average speed of the plane.
\newline

The diagram shows a conversion graph between dollars and Japanese yen.
\newline

A watch costs \$100.
\newline

Find the cost of this watch in yen.
\newline

The family go to a restaurant.
\newline

The total cost of the food and drinks is \$154.
\newline

The ratio cost of food : cost of drinks $= 21 : 4$.
\newline

Work out the cost of drinks.
\newline

\newline

8\% tax is then added to the cost of the holiday.
\newline

Find the total cost of the holiday, including tax, for the Perez family.
\newline

The plane takes 11 hours 40 minutes to fly from Los Angeles to Tokyo.
\newline

\newline

The local time in Tokyo is 17 hours ahead of the local time in Los Angeles.
\newline

\newline

\newline

Calculate the average speed of the plane.
\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

The family go to a restaurant.
\newline

The total cost of the food and drinks is \$154.
\newline

The ratio cost of food : cost of drinks $= 21 : 4$.
\newline

\newline

stem.content:
Mr and Mrs Perez and their 3 children go on holiday to Tokyo.
\newline

_rawBody:
Mr and Mrs Perez and their 3 children go on holiday to Tokyo.

\begin{parts}
\part The holiday costs \$3800 for an adult and \$2400 for a child.

8\% tax is then added to the cost of the holiday.

Find the total cost of the holiday, including tax, for the Perez family.

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{4}

\part The plane takes 11 hours 40 minutes to fly from Los Angeles to Tokyo.

The plane leaves on Wednesday at 10\,35 local time.

The local time in Tokyo is 17 hours ahead of the local time in Los Angeles.

\begin{subparts}
\subpart Find the day and local time in Tokyo when the plane arrives.

\AnswerLine[Day \makebox[2cm]{\hrulefill} \quad Time \makebox[2cm]{\hrulefill}][]
\Marks{3}

\subpart The distance the plane flies is 8820\,km.

Calculate the average speed of the plane.

\AnswerLine[][km/h]
\Marks{2}

\end{subparts}

\part The diagram shows a conversion graph between dollars and Japanese yen.

A watch costs \$100.

Find the cost of this watch in yen.

\AnswerLine[][yen]
\Marks{2}

\part The family go to a restaurant.

The total cost of the food and drinks is \$154.

The ratio cost of food : cost of drinks $= 21 : 4$.

Work out the cost of drinks.

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{2}

\end{parts}

qtype: building algebraic expressions, forming and solving linear equations, solving simultaneous linear equations
tex:
(a) 
Apples cost $\$a$ per kilogram and bananas cost $\$b$ per kilogram.
\newline

Lee buys $6\,\text{kg}$ of apples and $8\,\text{kg}$ of bananas.
\newline

Write down an expression, in terms of $a$ and $b$, for the total cost, in dollars, of the apples and the bananas.
\newline

Cara is one year older than twice Asher's age.
\newline

Brice is 22 years older than Asher.
\newline

The total of their three ages is 167.
\newline

Find the age of each person.
\newline

Solve the simultaneous equations.
\newline

You must show all your working.
\newline
\begin{align*}
3x + 2y &= 21 \\
2x - 5y &= 33
\end{align*}

content.content:
Apples cost $\$a$ per kilogram and bananas cost $\$b$ per kilogram.
\newline

Lee buys $6\,\text{kg}$ of apples and $8\,\text{kg}$ of bananas.
\newline

Write down an expression, in terms of $a$ and $b$, for the total cost, in dollars, of the apples and the bananas.
\newline

Cara is one year older than twice Asher's age.
\newline

Brice is 22 years older than Asher.
\newline

The total of their three ages is 167.
\newline

Find the age of each person.
\newline

Solve the simultaneous equations.
\newline

You must show all your working.
\newline
\begin{align*}
3x + 2y &= 21 \\
2x - 5y &= 33
\end{align*}

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}
\part Apples cost $\$a$ per kilogram and bananas cost $\$b$ per kilogram.

Lee buys 6\,kg of apples and 8\,kg of bananas.

Write down an expression, in terms of $a$ and $b$, for the total cost, in dollars, of the apples and the bananas.

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{2}

\part Cara is one year older than twice Asher's age.

Brice is 22 years older than Asher.

The total of their three ages is 167.

Find the age of each person.

\AnswerLine[Asher][years]
\AnswerLine[Brice][years]
\AnswerLine[Cara][years]
\Marks{4}

\part Solve the simultaneous equations.

You must show all your working.
\begin{align*}
3x + 2y &= 21 \\
2x - 5y &= 33
\end{align*}

\AnswerLine[$x =$][]
\AnswerLine[$y =$][]
\Marks{4}

\end{parts}

tex:

 flag $F$ onto flag $B$,

\newline

On the grid, draw the image of flag $F$ after a translation by the vector $\displaystyle\binom{3}{-4}$.
\newline

tex:

t

\hline
$x$ & $-5$ & $-4$ & $-3$ & $-2$ & $-1$ & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 \\
\hline
$y$ & 11 & & $-1$ & & & $-1$ & & & 11 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
